$schema: Once Upon A Time — UX recommendations for PDF-source pages
summary: Forty-eight page-level + eight cross-page UX recommendations for the pages already covered by the original PDF design set. These are improvements to existing surfaces, distinct from the off-spec inventory which catalogues net-new features.
audience: Designers picking up the project for the Shopify migration phase. Each recommendation leads with user reasoning, not implementation detail.

heuristics.emergency: Emergency usability — can the primary task be completed in 3 seconds, one-handed, on mobile?
heuristics.accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA — alt text, focus rings, contrast, heading hierarchy, dialog roles, skip links
heuristics.responsive: Responsive breakpoints — does the page hold up at 375 / 768 / 1280 / 1440?
heuristics.discovery: Search & discovery — can users find what they need? Is the entry point clear?
heuristics.errors: Error & offline — what happens when the form fails, the network drops, the action errors?
heuristics.emotional: Emotional & trust design — does the tone feel like the brand? Is micro-copy human or legalese?

page_recommendations.title: Hero carousel needs spoken status updates
page_recommendations.design_take: Make the carousel narrate itself — parents on assistive tech are missing featured programs.
page_recommendations.explanation: The hero rotates through four featured programs but a screen reader sees a static image. Parents using VoiceOver or TalkBack don't know the slide changed. Adding aria-live announcements ("Slide 2 of 4: Seasonal Camps") puts everyone on the same footing during program discovery.

page_recommendations.title: Today's classes need visual urgency cues
page_recommendations.design_take: Distinguish full from filling-fast at a glance — parents are deciding in 3 seconds.
page_recommendations.explanation: Right now every time-slot looks equally available. A parent scrolling at school pickup wants to know which class is filling fast and which is open. A small enrollment badge or coloured pill (Open · Filling · Full) earns its keep here.

page_recommendations.title: Donor testimonials need verification context
page_recommendations.design_take: A name and a quote isn't enough. Show why this person is credible.
page_recommendations.explanation: Major donors land on the home page first and judge organisational stewardship from quotes within seconds. Adding a small verified marker (years supported, or "Donor since 2022") signals these are real humans, not stock copy.

page_recommendations.title: Make enrolment the loud CTA on mobile
page_recommendations.design_take: Donate is shouting at the same volume as Enrol. Quiet one of them.
page_recommendations.explanation: Most homepage traffic is parents with kids in tow, not donors. Right now Donate and Enrol carry equal visual weight on mobile, which fragments intent. Demoting Donate to a secondary CTA (text link or outline button) on small screens lets parents find the path they came for.

page_recommendations.title: Values grid feels like a corporate report on mobile
page_recommendations.design_take: Three abstract value statements stacked vertically is a wall of text. Add an emotional anchor.
page_recommendations.explanation: Kids ages 6–18 do see this page (parents bring them along during decisions). Pairing each value with one illustrative photo or a child's quote makes the values feel like a story, not a brand pillar slide.

page_recommendations.title: Leadership names without role context = wasted real estate
page_recommendations.design_take: Yasmine, Dolores — who? Adults assessing credibility scroll for context.
page_recommendations.explanation: Headshots without roles read as "meet the team" filler. A single line under each name ("Executive Director · 12 years in Queens arts ed") lets parents and donors do a one-pass credibility check without scrolling.

page_recommendations.title: Success-stories carousel doesn't work without a mouse
page_recommendations.design_take: If your trackpad-less colleague can't read the testimonials, neither can a screen-reader user.
page_recommendations.explanation: The horizontal carousel lacks Previous / Next buttons that work via keyboard. Visible arrow controls with focus rings let everyone — keyboard users, low-vision users, screen readers — get through the stories.

page_recommendations.title: Newsletter signup hides the value exchange
page_recommendations.design_take: Tell people what they're getting before asking for their email.
page_recommendations.explanation: Right now the form just says "Subscribe". One sentence — "Weekly notes from the studio: class highlights, photos from showcases, and the occasional behind-the-scenes" — gives parents the trust signal they need to opt in.

page_recommendations.title: Phone number needs to be the loudest thing on this page
page_recommendations.design_take: If a parent's child forgot pickup time, they don't fill out a form — they call.
page_recommendations.explanation: Contact-us moments are often time-sensitive (forgot pickup, allergy concern, late drop-off). The phone number should be the first thing visible, with a prominent treatment — large display type or a tap-to-call button on mobile — not a list item alongside email and address.

page_recommendations.title: Form errors should announce themselves above the fold
page_recommendations.design_take: A parent who just hit Send and saw nothing happen will leave.
page_recommendations.explanation: Inline field errors are easy to miss, especially after a submission attempt scrolls the form out of view. A summary box at the top of the form on submission failure ("Please check 2 fields below") gives clear feedback and disables the submit button until corrections are made.

page_recommendations.title: FAQ chevrons need a visible open/closed state
page_recommendations.design_take: Keyboard users tabbing through FAQs can't tell which one is open.
page_recommendations.explanation: The native details/summary element handles state correctly but the visual treatment doesn't reinforce it strongly. A clear chevron rotation and a focus ring make the interaction legible to keyboard and low-vision users.

page_recommendations.title: Donation amounts need impact anchoring
page_recommendations.design_take: $50 is just a number. "$50 = a semester of dance for one child" is a decision.
page_recommendations.explanation: Preset amount buttons currently look like radio options. Adding a one-line impact statement under each ($25 = supplies for a workshop · $50 = a semester for one child · $250 = a private lesson series for a kid on scholarship) turns abstract dollars into concrete outcomes.

page_recommendations.title: Tabs collapse poorly on mobile
page_recommendations.design_take: Home / Saved / Read Later / Get Involved truncates and the active tab gets lost.
page_recommendations.explanation: Equal-width tab buttons cram together at 375px. Either go to a horizontal scroll-snap row with one fully-visible active tab, or collapse to a select dropdown on mobile so the user sees their current section clearly.

page_recommendations.title: Empty-search state is invisible
page_recommendations.design_take: Articles disappear with no explanation — looks like the page broke.
page_recommendations.explanation: When the filter returns zero matches, the grid silently empties. Add a centred "No articles match 'jazz'" state with a Clear-Search button. Reassures users that the system is working and gives them a clear way out.

page_recommendations.title: Bookmark state needs a sync indicator
page_recommendations.design_take: Bookmarks live in localStorage today. If a parent expects them on their other device, they'll be disappointed.
page_recommendations.explanation: Right now there's no signal that bookmarks are device-local. Either show a small "Saved locally" badge with a "Sign in to sync" CTA, or commit to syncing post-Shopify and show pending state during the round trip.

page_recommendations.title: Related-newsletters cards have stingy tap areas
page_recommendations.design_take: Make the whole card clickable, not just the title link.
page_recommendations.explanation: On mobile a kid (or a thumb-fumbling adult) is going to miss-tap. Wrapping the entire card in the anchor — title, image, summary — gives a 250px+ tap target that matches the iOS/Android guidelines.

page_recommendations.title: Drop cap should not be read out twice
page_recommendations.design_take: Screen readers can announce the drop-cap letter as a separate element. Hide it from them.
page_recommendations.explanation: If the article's first paragraph starts with a styled drop cap built via ::first-letter or a wrapper span, ensure the styled letter is `aria-hidden` and the actual paragraph reads naturally. Otherwise low-vision users hear "T. The dance studio is…".

page_recommendations.title: Share / Save / Print icons need text labels at all sizes
page_recommendations.design_take: Three icons with no text is a guess on mobile.
page_recommendations.explanation: Even small icons should carry visible labels at common reading distances. Stack the label under the icon at all viewports, or make this a button bar with text + icon. Cuts confusion between Save (bookmark) vs Print.

page_recommendations.title: Pull-quotes don't read as pull-quotes
page_recommendations.design_take: A grey left border isn't enough — it just looks like indented text.
page_recommendations.explanation: A bigger typography step, a tinted background, or a leading quote-mark glyph turns a blockquote into the visual breath the article needs. Right now they slip past in a scan.

page_recommendations.title: Filter chips need a clearer selected state
page_recommendations.design_take: aria-pressed is great for screen readers but invisible to eyes.
page_recommendations.explanation: When a parent taps Dance, the chip should change visually — solid fill, contrasted text — not just announce the change to assistive tech. Two-state styling (light unselected, brand-coloured selected) gives instant feedback.

page_recommendations.title: Empty filter results need a recovery message
page_recommendations.design_take: Cards just vanish — the parent thinks they broke the page.
page_recommendations.explanation: When chip + search combine to zero results, render an inviting "Nothing matches yet — try clearing filters or browsing Coming Soon" with a Clear-Filters button. Turns a dead end into a path forward.

page_recommendations.title: Enrolment status needs colour, not just text
page_recommendations.design_take: "3 spots left" is the difference between buying now and waiting. Make it shout.
page_recommendations.explanation: Current implementation displays capacity as plain text. Add a coloured pill (green = open, amber = filling, red = full or waitlist) plus an enrolment-percent bar in the card. Parents scan dozens of cards; visual shorthand wins.

page_recommendations.title: Parent testimonials need a verified marker
page_recommendations.design_take: Stock-looking quotes lose readers. Show this parent's kid is in the program.
page_recommendations.explanation: A small "Parent of Story Jazz student, 2024" line under each quote — with a verified checkmark — lifts these from copywriting to social proof. Especially important for a small nonprofit competing with big-brand studios.

page_recommendations.title: Sticky sidebar collides with content on mobile
page_recommendations.design_take: On a phone the sticky sidebar covers the section it's meant to navigate to.
page_recommendations.explanation: Convert the sidebar to a non-sticky table-of-contents card at the top of the page on mobile, or to a collapsible drawer triggered by a tap. Otherwise scrolling to FAQs hides the FAQ heading behind the nav.

page_recommendations.title: The three things parents need first are buried
page_recommendations.design_take: Capacity, price, dates. Surface them above the fold.
page_recommendations.explanation: A parent comparing camps does a 3-second pass for: when, how much, are there spots. Right now those facts live in a sidebar card mid-page. Promote them to a Quick Facts strip directly under the hero so the page rewards a quick scan.

page_recommendations.title: Instructor cards need a teaching philosophy line
page_recommendations.design_take: Patient & playful vs. rigorous & precise — parents pick a fit, not a credential.
page_recommendations.explanation: A photo and a name doesn't help a parent who's trying to figure out if this teacher is right for their nervous 8-year-old. One sentence of pedagogy under the name ("Patient, movement-first, builds confidence over technique") earns trust faster than a degree list.

page_recommendations.title: Studio location SVG is invisible to screen readers
page_recommendations.design_take: The pin icon needs to speak the address.
page_recommendations.explanation: Decorative SVGs need an aria-label or wrapping link so the address is reachable from assistive tech. Without it, a low-vision parent can't locate the studio without manual copy/paste of the text address.

page_recommendations.title: Expanded FAQ should scroll into view
page_recommendations.design_take: Tapping the bottom FAQ on a phone hides the answer.
page_recommendations.explanation: On mobile, opening a details/summary panel near the bottom of the viewport doesn't scroll the answer into view. A short scrollIntoView({behavior: smooth, block: nearest}) on toggle solves it.

page_recommendations.title: Tell parents how instructor matching works
page_recommendations.design_take: "How We Match You" answers the question parents are afraid to ask.
page_recommendations.explanation: Private lessons are a higher-trust, higher-cost commitment. The page should say plainly: do parents request an instructor, do staff match, can they switch? A short How-We-Match-You section reduces enrolment hesitation.

page_recommendations.title: Show package tiers, not a single price
page_recommendations.design_take: 4 lessons or 8? Save how much? Don't make the parent calculate.
page_recommendations.explanation: A simple comparison table (4 lessons / 8 lessons / 12 lessons with the per-lesson rate at each tier) lets parents commit at the right level without doing math.

page_recommendations.title: Show available time slots in-page
page_recommendations.design_take: Asynchronous "contact us to check" friction kills bookings.
page_recommendations.explanation: Even a read-only calendar of available slots (greyed-out booked, highlighted available) sets expectations and lets parents self-qualify before they reach out. After Shopify go-live this becomes a real-time booking widget.

page_recommendations.title: Role toggle needs a one-liner explanation
page_recommendations.design_take: Student / Parent or Donor — what's the difference and why does it matter?
page_recommendations.explanation: First-time users may pick the wrong role and end up in the wrong dashboard. A short caption under the toggle ("Parents enrol kids and view schedules. Donors manage contributions.") prevents the confusion and the support ticket.

page_recommendations.title: Password reveal icon fails contrast
page_recommendations.design_take: The eye icon disappears against the input on some breakpoints.
page_recommendations.explanation: Make sure the toggle's icon meets the WCAG AA 4.5:1 minimum against the input background — particularly important for parents using glasses to enter a long password.

page_recommendations.title: Social-login buttons need text labels
page_recommendations.design_take: Three icons in a row is a memory test, not a UI.
page_recommendations.explanation: "Continue with Google" / "Continue with Apple" / "Continue with Email" — full text labels — convert better than icons alone, especially with older parents.

page_recommendations.title: Forgot Password needs a hint about what happens next
page_recommendations.design_take: Clicking the link is scary if you don't know whether it deletes your account.
page_recommendations.explanation: A small caption ("We'll email you a reset link") underneath disambiguates. Also a confirmation toast after submission so users know the email was sent and can check their inbox.

page_recommendations.title: Sign-up success state is missing
page_recommendations.design_take: Did the form submit? The user has no idea.
page_recommendations.explanation: After submitting, swap the form for a clear success panel: "We've sent a confirmation email to alex@example.com. Check your inbox to verify." Eliminates the "did it work?" double-tap that creates duplicate accounts.

page_recommendations.title: First / Last name fields cramp on tablet
page_recommendations.design_take: Two fields side-by-side at 768px = wrapped placeholder text.
page_recommendations.explanation: Stack the name fields vertically below 900px wide. Names like "Maria-Jose Hernandez-Smith" don't fit comfortably in half a tablet width.

page_recommendations.title: Switching Card → PayPal should change the form visibly
page_recommendations.design_take: Right now selecting PayPal still shows card fields. Confusing.
page_recommendations.explanation: Hide the card-specific fields when PayPal is selected (and reveal the PayPal CTA). Otherwise users fill in a card form they didn't intend to use, get confused on submit.

page_recommendations.title: Billing address fields should reflow by country
page_recommendations.design_take: State dropdown for someone in the UK is wrong (and embarrassing).
page_recommendations.explanation: Country first, then conditionally render state/province / postal-code fields with the right label and validation for that country. Critical for international donors.

page_recommendations.title: CVV needs a tiny explainer
page_recommendations.design_take: Older parents skip the field because they don't know what it is.
page_recommendations.explanation: A help icon next to CVV that shows a small "3-digit code on the back of your card" tooltip prevents abandonment. Every checkout team learns this eventually — get it right from the start.

page_recommendations.title: Generic impact stats miss the moment
page_recommendations.design_take: "50K+ Lives Impacted" sounds corporate. Show what THIS donation does.
page_recommendations.explanation: On the payment screen — the highest-emotion moment of the donor flow — replace generic stats with a personalised line: "Your $50 sponsors a semester of theatre for one student." Much harder to walk away from.

page_recommendations.title: Account sidebar pushes content too far down on mobile
page_recommendations.design_take: On a phone, the sidebar takes up the entire viewport before content begins.
page_recommendations.explanation: Convert the side nav to a slim tab bar across the top, or a collapsible drawer triggered by a hamburger. Saves a half-screen of mobile real estate.

page_recommendations.title: Child profile cards lack edit / remove
page_recommendations.design_take: Today: parents have to call support to fix a typo in their kid's name.
page_recommendations.explanation: Each child card needs Edit and Remove buttons. Self-service for typos, allergy updates, and de-enrolment when a child ages out. Reduces support load and frustration.

page_recommendations.title: Account Security section should show 2FA status at a glance
page_recommendations.design_take: Security settings without a status badge is an invitation to ignore them.
page_recommendations.explanation: Beside each row (email, password, two-factor) show "Enabled" in green or "Set up" as a CTA. Lets users complete the security checklist without clicking through.

page_recommendations.title: Critical notification toggles need confirmation
page_recommendations.design_take: Accidental tap = parent stops getting class reminders. They miss class.
page_recommendations.explanation: For high-impact notification toggles (class reminders, payment alerts), the first time a user disables them, show a small confirm dialog: "You'll stop receiving class reminders. Are you sure?" Saves the parent and the kid.

page_recommendations.title: Photo / video consent needs context-specific toggles
page_recommendations.design_take: Some parents are fine with internal records, not website features. Don't force all-or-nothing.
page_recommendations.explanation: Split the consent into two toggles — "For internal class records" vs "For website / social media". Privacy-conscious parents shouldn't have to opt out of all photos to keep their kid off Instagram.

page_recommendations.title: Delete Account needs the destructive treatment
page_recommendations.design_take: A subtle button is the wrong UI for an irreversible action.
page_recommendations.explanation: Distinct red styling, a confirmation modal that requires typing the word DELETE or the user's email, and a clear list of what gets erased (enrolment history, payment methods, child profiles). Standard pattern for a reason.

page_recommendations.title: Status badges should not rely on colour alone
page_recommendations.design_take: Colour-blind users can't distinguish Starting Soon from Closed.
page_recommendations.explanation: Each badge needs a text label inside (Starting Soon · Active · Completed · Cancelled). Colour reinforces but never replaces.

page_recommendations.title: Action links need a primary
page_recommendations.design_take: View Details, View Receipt, Cancel, Report Issue — they all look equally important.
page_recommendations.explanation: View Details is the primary action 90% of the time. Make it a button, demote the others to text-link secondary actions. Fewer choices = faster decisions.

page_recommendations.title: Cancellation outcome should be explicit
page_recommendations.design_take: Tapping Cancel and seeing nothing happen is terrifying for a parent.
page_recommendations.explanation: After a confirmed cancellation: toast ("Class cancelled. Refund processed within 5 business days."), card visually moves to a Cancelled section, refund timeline shown. Three reassurances at once.

cross_page_recommendations.title: Define ONE empty-state pattern and use it everywhere
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: Programs, Newsletter, Class History, Search — all silently empty. Pick one welcoming pattern.
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Every list / grid / search across the site needs the same "no results" component: friendly headline, one suggestion, one Clear-Filter button. Not having this leaves four pages where users think the site broke.

cross_page_recommendations.title: Show sync status on every authenticated change
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: If the user just toggled something, tell them it saved.
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Account preferences, child profiles, payment methods — every change today is silent. A small Saving… → Saved indicator (or a toast) consistently across all account pages eliminates the "did that work?" anxiety.

cross_page_recommendations.title: Quick-Facts strip on every program / commitment page
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: Three-second scan: when, how much, are spots open. Nothing else first.
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Index, Programs, Seasonal Camps, Private Lessons all need the same compact strip directly under the hero: dates, price (or down payment), capacity, age range. Same component, same position. Parents shouldn't relearn the page.

cross_page_recommendations.title: One trust-marker pattern for testimonials and credentials
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: If we can't verify it, why is it on the page?
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Every testimonial — donor on the home page, parent on programs, leader on About — needs the same small verification cue (verified checkmark, role, year of involvement). Without it, the cumulative effect is "these are quotes from a marketing draft".

cross_page_recommendations.title: Sticky sidebars don't work on mobile — pick one collapse pattern
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: Today the sidebar covers content on phones. Designers need to spec the small-screen behaviour.
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Detail pages, account pages — multiple uses of the sticky sidebar pattern. Pick one — top tab strip, hamburger drawer, or non-sticky TOC card — and use it everywhere. The current inconsistency confuses navigation muscle memory.

cross_page_recommendations.title: Role context everywhere account creation appears
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: Student vs Donor isn't obvious. Tell people why it matters.
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Login, signup, and any role-switch UI need the same one-line explanation: "Parents enrol kids and view class schedules. Donors manage contributions." One paragraph saves dozens of support tickets.

cross_page_recommendations.title: Single accessible-carousel pattern
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: Every carousel today is a different a11y story. Pick one.
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Hero on home, Success Stories on About, possibly more — all need: visible Previous/Next buttons (keyboard-reachable), aria-live on slide change, dot indicators with 24×24 hit targets. One spec, used everywhere.

cross_page_recommendations.title: Destructive-action pattern site-wide
cross_page_recommendations.design_take: Delete, Cancel, Remove — every one needs the same red, the same modal.
cross_page_recommendations.explanation: Account deletion, class cancellation, removing a payment method — same visual language: red CTA, confirmation modal, list of consequences. Standardising means users learn it once.
